items.title: How have I never heard of this game until now?
items.summary: Morimen’s is great game for three reason, even though it’s a gacha spending isn’t everything and any character can work with enough effort. The pull rates are stellar compared to any modern gacha. The gameplay is challenging enough to be absolutely addicting. Please work on you all’s marketing though this game should be much more popular than it is
items.author: Kalpat64

items.title: One of the best games I've played
items.summary: The character designs are impressive, and their story is fascinating. I would love to interact with them more. The gacha system is okay, and I had so much fun pulling for characters. The game mechanics are very fun! Personally, I’d love to dive deeper into the main story and the characters’ pasts.
items.author: Mafmafion

items.title: Great Game, 10/10 Artwork
items.summary: Truly a great game, very fair in acquiring characters and currency! The translation is almost perfect so it’s easy to play on English aswell. The storytelling is insane and there’s no need to skip it at all.
items.author: rowentw

items.title: It turned 17+ 🥀
items.summary:
The game is wonderful, and I can tell the developers care about. The art, the animation and music are beautiful and the character designs are unique and refreshing. I really love the turn based card and dungeon crawl feature as well—it’s probably my favorite, and I have genuine fun with it, as there is definitely a risk of losing, no matter if you have “rare” characters or not, as it all trickles down to technique and knowing the enemy. It doesn’t rely on the old, “Full team of ssr” characters or whatever to progress, which makes me excited when I do story stages. Whenever I’m stuck, it’s almost always due to the strategy I used, rather than the team power.

And yet. It turned 17+💔

I was elated when I had seen that the 2nd anniversary began, and quickly downloaded the update. But then, I saw the ratings. I only wondered what drastic changes the second anniversary could have brought until I read the list for the rating. “Infrequent horror” “infrequent medical treatment” “infrequent drugs and alcohol” 
Devs, if you read this, I ask for a real response. Why, in the world, did you find the need to include, “infrequent horror”, when the medical and fantasy violences is already there? And, I must know, what the drug and tobacco reference is. Is it completely separate from the medical treatment, or are you adding both tags to the same instance? Perhaps this is a little much for me to go write a review, however, my account won’t show this game till I turn eighteen. I know very well that within that time my account has EXTREMELY high chances of deleting itself. Although I don’t know the backup system too well, I know that mistakes can happen, and I would be devastated to find my account with all of my favorite characters deleted. I’m also aware that the chances of you catering to my ask are low, and I have a feeling that the team discussed this and acted upon, even considering what I’m about to say. Yet, I ask you to consider this once more. By changing the games rating to 17+, you are not only shrinking your possible audience, but inconveniencing those of us who have already spent our time and money. I have no doubt that other’s have also experienced this, which is the sole reason why my review is 3*. If not for that, a full star review, no doubt. And, if you manage to read this review, I further ask you if the changes could be made before the 2nd anniversary ends, if you do ever consider this. 
       Best regards, whether the changes are made or not, as I still love this game dearly, and wish it’s future, and the developers, the best.
items.author: Ophelia150
